summary:
Optic atrophy results from ischemic or toxic injury to retinal neurons or the optic nerve, as seen in retinitis pigmentosa, methanol中毒, and central retinal artery occlusion. Polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy predominantly affects choroidal blood vessels without inducing neurodegeneration or optic nerve atrophy.  

The correct choice is therefore $\boxed{\text{D}}$. 

(Only one summary sentence needed per requirement.)

Corrected Summary based solely on core clinical facts:  
Polypoidal choroidal vasculopathy does not cause optic atrophy because it involves choroidal neovasculature without damaging retinal neurons or the optic nerve. In contrast, diseases such as retinitis pigmentosa, methanol intoxication, and CRAO induce optic atrophy through mechanisms involving neuron loss or ischemia.
